name: Tuna-Broccoli Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrub potatoes clean and prick the skins with a fork. Rub each potato all over with vegetable oil.
2. Bake in a 425°F (220°C) oven for 45 to 55 minutes, or until the potatoes yield to gentle pressure. Alternatively, microwave the potatoes.
3. While the potatoes are cooking, bring a large pot of water to boil and cook broccoli for 2 to 3 minutes, or until tender-crisp. Drain and rinse under cold water, then drain well. Chop coarsely and place in a large bowl.
4. Cleave the tuna can; flake the tuna into the bowl with the broccoli mixture, celery, and chopped green onions.
5. In another bowl, combine mayonnaise, sour cream, and mustard (Dijon). Set aside.
6. Cut each potato in half lengthwise, leaving a 1/4 inch (5 mm) thick shell on one side. Brush the skins with butter and place them on a baking sheet; keep warm.
7. In a bowl, mash the potato pulp. Add the broccoli mixture and 3/4 cup (175 ml) of the mayonnaise mixture to the mashed potatoes. Stir in cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper to taste.
8. Stuff each potato shell with the potato-filling mixture, mounding the tops slightly. Return to the oven for an additional 15 minutes, or until steaming hot. Alternatively, microwave the filled potatoes.
9. Serve with remaining mayonnaise mixture spooned over each potato.
